body_txt: One of the common Moon myths in Western culture is the saying that it is made of "green" note originally meaning "young" cheese. There was never a time when this was a popular belief, though people sometimes talk as if there really was once a mythic culture credulous enough to believe it . This can manifest in many ways in fiction, often for laughs . For example, the moon may be depicted as made of Cartoon Cheese (possibly even a shade of green), and it may even be inhabited by moon mice (since mice love cheese and all that). Often, the cheese closely resembles the varieties most commonly used for Cartoon Cheese , particularly Cheddar for its pale yellow-white colour, and Emmental, as the holes translate well into representing craters on the lunar surface. In Video Games , this can overlap with Level Ate as applied to lunar settings. See also The Man in the Moon , the other common Western folklore about the moon. The closest Eastern equivalent would be the Moon Rabbit , which traditionally pounds mochi . Any cheese taken from such a moon would qualify as Fantastic Fromage .
